title: DEQ to Exercise Enforcement Discretion for 2026 Model Year Under Oregonâ€™s Advanced Clean Cars II Rule
content:
Late Friday,Â Oregon Department of Environmental QualityÂ Director Leah Feldon issued aÂ memoÂ directing the agency to exercise limited enforcement discretion for Oregonâ€™sÂ Advanced Clean Cars II Rule, also known as ACC II. Effective immediately, DEQ will not pursue enforcement or assess penalties against vehicle manufacturers that do not meet zero-emissions vehicle sales targets for Model Year 2026 passenger cars and light-duty trucks.

The ACC II regulation,Â adoptedÂ by the Oregon Environmental Quality Commission in 2022, requires auto manufacturers to sell an increasing percentage of zero-emission vehicles and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles beginning with model year 2026. This program is a key part of Oregonâ€™s strategy to improve air quality, protect public health and reduce greenhouse gas emissions from transportation. Recent federal actions, including a Congressional vote to revoke Californiaâ€™s Clean Air Act waiver for this rule, have created significant uncertainty for the regulated manufacturers. In response, California, Oregon, nine other states and the District of Columbia have filed aÂ legal challengeÂ to defend Californiaâ€™s waivers and the statesâ€™ long-standing authority to adopt clean vehicle standards under the Clean Air Act. Given the current legal issues, DEQ recognizes that auto manufacturers face a complex and rapidly evolving regulatory environment. This enforcement discretion is intended to provide clarity and stability for the 2026 model year while ensuring continued availability of vehicles for Oregon consumers and businesses. Be sure to sign up throughÂ DEQâ€™s GovDelivery systemÂ to receive the latest information on these changes by email.
author: Mansift Kaur
source: State of Oregon Department of Environment Quality
